title: Camera Trap Data Package
description: Data exchange format for camera trap data. <https://camtrap-dp.tdwg.org>

allOf.title: Data Package
allOf.description: Data Package is a simple specification for data access and delivery.

profile.title: Profile
profile.context: Every Package and Resource descriptor has a profile. The default profile, if none is declared, is `data-package` for Package and `data-resource` for Resource.

name.title: Name
name.description: An identifier string. Lower case characters with `.`, `_`, `-` and `/` are allowed.
name.context: This is ideally a url-usable and human-readable name. Name `SHOULD` be invariant, meaning it `SHOULD NOT` change when its parent descriptor is updated.

id.title: ID
id.description: A property reserved for globally unique identifiers. Examples of identifiers that are unique include UUIDs and DOIs.
id.context: A common usage pattern for Data Packages is as a packaging format within the bounds of a system or platform. In these cases, a unique identifier for a package is desired for common data handling workflows, such as updating an existing package. While at the level of the specification, global uniqueness cannot be validated, consumers using the `id` property `MUST` ensure identifiers are globally unique.

description.title: Description
description.description: A text description. Markdown is encouraged.

homepage.title: Home Page
homepage.description: The home on the web that is related to this data package.

created.title: Created
created.description: The datetime on which this descriptor was created.
created.context: The datetime must conform to the string formats for datetime as described in [RFC3339](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)

items.title: Contributor

path.title: Path
path.description: A fully qualified URL, or a POSIX file path.
path.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

organization.title: Organization
organization.description: An organizational affiliation for this contributor.

items.context: Use of this property does not imply that the person was the original creator of, or a contributor to, the data in the descriptor, but refers to the composition of the descriptor itself.

keywords.title: Keywords
keywords.description: A list of keywords that describe this package.

image.title: Image
image.description: A image to represent this package.

licenses.title: Licenses
licenses.description: The license(s) under which this package is published.

items.title: License

name.title: Open Definition license identifier
name.description: MUST be an Open Definition license identifier, see http://licenses.opendefinition.org/

path.title: Path
path.description: A fully qualified URL, or a POSIX file path.
path.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

items.context: Use of this property does not imply that the person was the original creator of, or a contributor to, the data in the descriptor, but refers to the composition of the descriptor itself.

licenses.context: This property is not legally binding and does not guarantee that the package is licensed under the terms defined herein.

resources.title: Data Resources
resources.description: An `array` of Data Resource objects, each compliant with the [Data Resource](/data-resource/) specification.

profile.title: Profile
profile.context: Every Package and Resource descriptor has a profile. The default profile, if none is declared, is `data-package` for Package and `data-resource` for Resource.

name.title: Name
name.description: An identifier string. Lower case characters with `.`, `_`, `-` and `/` are allowed.
name.context: This is ideally a url-usable and human-readable name. Name `SHOULD` be invariant, meaning it `SHOULD NOT` change when its parent descriptor is updated.

path.title: Path
path.description: A reference to the data for this resource, as either a path as a string, or an array of paths as strings. of valid URIs.

oneOf.title: Path
oneOf.description: A fully qualified URL, or a POSIX file path.
oneOf.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

items.title: Path
items.description: A fully qualified URL, or a POSIX file path.
items.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

path.context: The dereferenced value of each referenced data source in `path` `MUST` be commensurate with a native, dereferenced representation of the data the resource describes. For example, in a *Tabular* Data Resource, this means that the dereferenced value of `path` `MUST` be an array.

path.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

format.title: Format
format.description: The file format of this resource.

mediatype.title: Media Type
mediatype.description: The media type of this resource. Can be any valid media type listed with [IANA](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml).

encoding.title: Encoding
encoding.description: The file encoding of this resource.

bytes.title: Bytes
bytes.description: The size of this resource in bytes.

hash.title: Hash
hash.description: The MD5 hash of this resource. Indicate other hashing algorithms with the {algorithm}:{hash} format.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: The raw sources for this resource.

path.title: Path
path.description: A fully qualified URL, or a POSIX file path.
path.context: Implementations need to negotiate the type of path provided, and dereference the data accordingly.

resources.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#resource-information). Camtrap DP further requires each object to be a [Tabular Data Resource](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/tabular-data-resource/) with a specific `name` and `schema`. See [Data](https://camtrap-dp.tdwg.org/data) for the requirements for those resources.

path.description: Path or URL to the data file.

profile.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#profile). Camtrap DP further requires this to be the URL of the used Camtrap DP Profile version (e.g. `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tdwg/camtrap-dp/1.0/camtrap-dp-profile.json`).

created.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#created). Camtrap DP makes this a required property.

title.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#title). Not to be confused with the title of the project that originated the package (`package.project.title`).

contributors.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#contributors). Camtrap DP makes this a required property and restricts `role` values. Can include people and organizations.

role.description: Role of the contributor. Defaults to `contributor`.

description.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#description). Not to be confused with the description of the project that originated the package (`package.project.description`).

sources.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#sources). Can include the data management platform from which the package was derived (e.g. Agouti, Trapper, Wildlife Insights).

licenses.description: See [Data Package specification](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/data-package/#licenses). If provided, Camtrap DP further requires at least a license for the content of the package and one for the media files.

scope.description: Scope of the license. `data` applies to the content of the package and resources, `media` to the (locally or externally hosted) media files referenced in `media.filePath`.

project.description: Camera trap project or study that originated the package.

title.description: Title of the project. Not to be confused with the title of the package (`package.title`).

description.description: Description of the project. Preferably formatted as [Markdown](http://commonmark.org/). Not to be confused with the description of the package (`package.description`).

samplingDesign.description: Type of a sampling design/layout. The values are based on [Wearn & Glover-Kapfer (2017)](https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.23409.17767), pages 80-82: `simple random`: random distribution of sampling locations; `systematic random`: random distribution of sampling locations, but arranged in a regular pattern; `clustered random`: random distribution of sampling locations, but clustered in arrays; `experimental`: non-random distribution aimed to study an effect, including the before-after control-impact (BACI) design; `targeted`: non-random distribution optimized for capturing specific target species (often using various bait types); `opportunistic`: opportunistic camera trapping (usually with a small number of cameras).

captureMethod.description: Method(s) used to capture the media files.

classificationEffort.description: To what extent the media have been classified.

individualAnimals.description: `true` if the project includes marked or recognizable individuals. See also `observations.individualID`.

observationLevel.description: Level at which observations are provided. See also `observations.observationLevel`.

coordinatePrecision.description: Least precise coordinate precision of the `deployments.latitude` and `deployments.longitude` (e.g. `0.01` for coordinates with a precision of 0.01 and 0.001 degree). Especially relevant when coordinates have been rounded to protect sensitive species.

start.description: Start date of the first deployment. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

end.description: End date of the last (completed) deployment. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

taxonomic.description: Taxonomic coverage of the package, based on the unique `observations.scientificName`.

taxonID.description: Unique identifier of the taxon. Preferably a global unique identifier issued by an authoritative checklist.

taxonRank.description: Taxonomic rank of the scientific name.

kingdom.description: Kingdom in which the taxon is classified.

phylum.description: Phylum or division in which the taxon is classified

class.description: Class in which the taxon is classified.

order.description: Order in which the taxon is classified.

family.description: Family in which the taxon is classified.

genus.description: Genus in which the taxon is classified.

vernacularNames.description: Common or vernacular names of the taxon, as `languageCode: vernacular name` pairs. Language codes should follow ISO 693-3 (e.g. `eng` for English).

^[a-z]{3}$.description: Common or vernacular name of the taxon in that language.

relatedIdentifiers.description: Identifiers of resources related to the package (e.g. papers, project pages, derived datasets, APIs, etc.).

relationType.description: Description of the relationship between the resource (the package) and the related resource.

relatedIdentifier.description: Unique identifier of the related resource (e.g. a DOI or URL).

resourceTypeGeneral.description: General type of the related resource.

references.description: List of references related to the package (e.g. references cited in `package.project.description`). References preferably include a DOI.
